title: Permission error - mount permission denied - during building image or when running `buildah info` command
body:
### Issue Description

I am not able to build images in CI (Jenkins). Buildah fails with this error:

```
buildah build -t foobar/flyway:${GIT_COMMIT} SOME_RANDOM_DIR/.
Error: mount /storage/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay:/storage/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay, flags: 0x1000: permission denied
time="2025-07-10T08:40:05Z" level=warning msg="failed to shutdown storage: \"mount /storage/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay:/storage/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay, flags: 0x1000: permission denied\""
```

Also `buildah info` fails with:

```
$ buildah info
Error: overlay: failed to make mount private: mount /storage/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay:/storage/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay, flags: 0x1000: permission denied
WARN[0000] failed to shutdown storage: "overlay: failed to make mount private: mount /storage/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay:/storage/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay, flags: 0x1000: permission denied" 
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. We are trying to build container images in CI (Jenkins)
2. We are using self hosted kubernetes cluster (kOps) running on AWS. Jenkins is running on the same k8s cluster.
3. The build goes through if we use `privileged: true`.
4. Now, I am trying to build the image without `privileged: true`. I am using this configuration:

```
    - name: buildah
      image: foobar/buildah:latest
      tty: true
      command:
        - cat
      securityContext:
        runAsUser: 1000
        runAsGroup: 1000
        appArmorProfile:
          type: unconfined
      volumeMounts:
        - name: storage
          mountPath: /storage
        - name: storage-conf
          mountPath: /home/build/.config/containers/
      labels:
        agent: buildah
```

* The only change from upstream buildah is that we have installed `parallel` in buildah for parallelizing the builds. But I don't think that's important because `buildah info` command also complains:

```
$ buildah info
Error: overlay: failed to make mount private: mount /storage/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay:/storage/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay, flags: 0x1000: permission denied
WARN[0000] failed to shutdown storage: "overlay: failed to make mount private: mount /storage/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay:/storage/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay, flags: 0x1000: permission denied" 
```

5. This is my `storage-conf.yaml`:

```
$ cat storage-conf.yaml 
---
kind: ConfigMap
apiVersion: v1
metadata:
  name: storage-conf
data:
  storage.conf: |
    [storage]
    driver = "overlay"
    runroot = "/storage/run/containers/storage"
    graphroot = "/storage/.local/share/containers/storage"
    rootless_storage_path = "/storage/.local/share/containers/storage"
    [storage.options]
    pull_options = {enable_partial_images = "true", use_hard_links = "false", ostree_repos=""}
    [storage.options.overlay]
```

### Describe the results you received

The build fails with

```
Error: overlay: failed to make mount private: mount /storage/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay:/storage/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay, flags: 0x1000: permission denied
WARN[0000] failed to shutdown storage: "overlay: failed to make mount private: mount /storage/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay:/storage/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay, flags: 0x1000: permission denied" 
```

### Describe the results you expected

The build to go through or `buildah info` showing correct output.

### buildah version output

```yaml
$ buildah --version
buildah version 1.40.1 (image-spec 1.1.1, runtime-spec 1.2.1)
```

### buildah info output

```yaml
$ buildah info
Error: overlay: failed to make mount private: mount /storage/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay:/storage/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay, flags: 0x1000: permission denied
WARN[0000] failed to shutdown storage: "overlay: failed to make mount private: mount /storage/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay:/storage/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay, flags: 0x1000: permission denied"
```

### Provide your storage.conf

```toml
$ grep -v "#"  /usr/share/containers/storage.conf
[storage]
driver = "overlay"
runroot = "/run/containers/storage"
graphroot = "/var/lib/containers/storage"
[storage.options]
additionalimagestores = [
"/usr/lib/containers/storage",
]
pull_options = {enable_partial_images = "true", use_hard_links = "false", ostree_repos=""}
[storage.options.overlay]
mountopt = "nodev,metacopy=on"

$ grep -v "^#" /etc/containers/storage.conf
[storage]
driver = "overlay"
runroot = "/run/containers/storage"
graphroot = "/var/lib/containers/storage"
[storage.options]
additionalimagestores = [
"/var/lib/shared",
"/usr/lib/containers/storage",
]
pull_options = {enable_partial_images = "true", use_hard_links = "false", ostree_repos=""}
[storage.options.overlay]
mount_program = "/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs"
mountopt = "nodev,fsync=0"
```

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

```
$ uname -r
6.8.0-1030-aws
$ arch
x86_64
```

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: That 0x1000 is the storage library attempting to bind mount the graph root over itself so that it can mark mounts under it as private.  Normally, that should be allowed within the user namespace that it's set up for itself inside of the container.  Adding `skip_mount_home = "true"` to the storage configuration might be enough to work around that, or it may simply defer the error until it tries to do something else with that directory.  What sort of volume is it?
I'm not yet sure that there's going to be anything to patch here.  Converting to a discussion.
